task: T8d Conway 20x20 — amplification trend test (raw 38 long-term)
selftest: PASS — blinker period-2 invariant verified on 20x20

grid_size_amplification_trend.trend_interpretation: ★ NON-MONOTONIC — 5x5 → 10x10 AMPLIFIED (+4pp), but 10x10 → 20x20 DEGENERATED (-8pp). Sweet spot at 10x10.

honest_finding: Larger grid hypothesis FALSIFIED — amplification does NOT continue beyond 10x10. Reason: patterned fraction shrinks (4-cell pattern / 400 grid = 1% live density) → both models trivially predict 0 for majority empty cells → accuracy converges to baseline. Sweet spot is grid size where pattern occupies ~5-15% of cells (10x10 ≈ 4% but enough chaos to dominate).
verdict: POSITIVE_BUT_NOT_AMPLIFIED — overall trend NON-MONOTONIC across grid sizes
law_64_status_post_20x20: Conway 5x5+10x10+20x20 grid family confirms grid-size-conditional behavior. Defensible re-statement narrowed: 'CA(5) advantage on Conway substrate peaks at 10x10 grid (~9%); compressed on small grids (5x5 ~5%) and diluted on large grids (20x20 ~1%). Sweet spot domain-specific.'
raw_91_C3_honest: 20x20 patterned fraction = 1% live density → trivial 0-predict converges accuracy
Hypothesis 'larger grid amplifies advantage' was MY assumption per cycle 4 closure §5; falsified by this iter
Real finding: there IS amplification but with dilution ceiling — 10x10 sweet spot

raw_38_long_term_revised: 20x20 falsified extreme-grid hypothesis. New direction: variable-density init (more pattern cells per grid area) at 10x10 + 15x15 + 20x20 to test whether density (not grid size alone) is the true governing variable.
